per_question.question: Does prospective international cohort study demonstrate inability of interim PET to predict treatment failure in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma?
per_question.reference: This large international cohort delivers 3 novel findings: treatment response assessed by I-PET is comparable across disparate healthcare systems, secondly a negative I-PET findings together with good clinical status identifies a group with an EFS of 98%, and thirdly a single I-PET scan does not differentiate chemoresistant lymphoma from complete response and cannot be used to guide risk-adapted therapy.
per_question.answer: A prospective international cohort study published in the Journal of Clinical Oncology found that interim PET (Positron Emission Tomography) scans did not reliably predict treatment failure for patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma. The results suggest limitations to using interim PET as a sole predictor, indicating it may need additional clinical context or other biomarkers for accurate prognosis assessment.
